t_segments.topic: 计算机作为工具的本质与三次革命
t_segments.summary: 嘉宾用“思维的自行车”比喻计算机，阐述了其作为工具如何放大人类能力。回顾了电子表格和桌面出版带来的两次产业爆炸，并展望了以人际计算为核心的第三次革命。

t_segments.topic: 个人电脑的演进与NeXT的未来愿景
t_segments.summary: 嘉宾分享了首次接触电脑的经历，并将个人电脑比作汽车，赋予个人自由。指出早期电脑的“孤岛”问题，并介绍了NeXT通过强大计算、集成网络和全新软件架构解决这些挑战的愿景。

t_segments.topic: 人际计算的实践与家用电脑的未来
t_segments.summary: 嘉宾以NeXT内部项目为例，展示人际计算如何打破管理和地域界限，提高决策效率。探讨了网络如何定义家用电脑市场，并强调多媒体是实现更好沟通和培训的手段而非目的。

t_segments.topic: Macintosh的革命性界面、创造者精神与普及之路
t_segments.summary: 嘉宾回忆参观施乐PARC的经历，以及Macintosh如何通过图形界面成为“大众电脑”。强调了艺术与科学结合的“思考者即行动者”精神，并指出教育是推动电脑普及的关键。

t_segments.topic: 个人电脑的诞生与Apple II的商业成功
t_segments.summary: 嘉宾讲述了微处理器最初被误解为计算器，以及业余爱好者如何将其发展成电脑。回顾了Apple I和Apple II的诞生，强调了Apple II作为首个成功商业电脑，凭借可靠软盘和VisiCalc奠定行业基础。

t_segments.topic: 创新、市场研究与苹果的创业初心
t_segments.summary: 嘉宾指出市场研究在非增量创新中的局限性，强调突破性产品需依赖直觉。他解释了苹果并非出于创业意愿，而是因被大公司拒绝，才被迫成立公司，并最终意识到其巨大潜力。

t_segments.topic: NeXT的制造理念与计算机在教育领域的应用
t_segments.summary: 嘉宾阐述了NeXT工厂自动化是为了提升质量和缩短上市时间，而非单纯降低成本。回顾了高等教育在计算机网络应用方面的领先地位，并讨论了K-12教育中计算机普及面临的挑战。

t_segments.topic: Macintosh发布的情感时刻与核心理念回顾
t_segments.summary: 嘉宾深情回顾Macintosh发布时的激动人心，团队成员在产品外壳上签名，以及发布会上观众的起立鼓掌。最后快速总结了个人电脑的起源、用户界面的核心哲学以及网络通信的未来。

p_segments.question: 计算机如何像工具一样，大幅提升我们的能力极限？
p_segments.insight: 计算机的本质是'思维的自行车'，它能像自行车放大人的运动效率一样，将人类固有的能力放大到惊人的程度，远超我们自身固有的极限。

p_segments.question: 在快速变化的商业环境中，企业如何通过人际计算实现快速重组和高效协作？
p_segments.insight: 传统的层级和地域组织结构无法适应快速变化的商业环境，而通过网络连接的计算机可以快速创建和解散任务型协作小组，实现电子化重组，这是未来组织保持敏捷性的关键。

p_segments.question: 个人电脑的普及对社会和个人产生了哪些深远影响？
p_segments.insight: 个人电脑的出现如同汽车的发明，赋予了个人交通自由，它让数百万人在旧范式下提前数十年接触电脑，并能自主选择和掌控自己的命运，这是个人电脑革命最大的影响。
p_segments.transcript: and and certainly one needs to factor out the environmental concerns of the of the analogy as well there is a lot to be said for comparing it to going from trains from passenger trains to automobiles and the advent of the automobile gave us a personal freedom of transportation in the same way the advent of the computer gave us the ability to start to use computers without having to convince other people that we needed to use computers and the biggest effect of the personal computer revolution has been to allow millions and millions of people to experience computers themselves decades before they ever would have in the old paradigm and to allow them to participate in the making of choices and controlling
p_segments.quote: The biggest effect of the personal computer revolution has been to allow millions of people to experience computers themselves.

p_segments.question: 如何打造一个能够引领行业变革的下一代计算平台？
p_segments.insight: 要打造引领行业变革的计算平台，需在三大方面实现突破：提供更强大的计算能力，集成网络实现人际计算，以及创建全新的软件架构以将应用开发效率提升四倍，从而赋能新一代应用腾飞，成为新的行业标准。

p_segments.insight: 人际计算通过打破管理和地域界限，让管理者实时洞察公司内部的思考、分歧和决策过程，显著减少会议，提高决策质量和参与度。

p_segments.quoteCn: 我们发现会议数量至少减少了 50%，而决策质量却大大提高了。

p_segments.question: 设计产品时，如何平衡联网和独立使用的需求？
p_segments.insight: 尽管网络连接提供强大功能，但个人电脑仍需具备流畅的独立使用和脱网能力，以满足用户在无网络环境下的个性化需求，实现联网与独立模式的无缝切换。
p_segments.transcript: I'm part of this electronically connected community that's going to provide us wonderful new capabilities and communications abilities but we still always want to be able to disconnect that network spigot take it off and take our standalone computer somewhere let's say home now what's going to happen rapidly is with radio links and with fiber optics to the home you're going to be able to hook your computer up to your network at home ah but there's always going to be that cabin in the middle of nowhere that I want to go for a two-week vacation where I want my computer and if it doesn't work in a completely standalone way I'm I'm going to be not happy so we have provide a fluid way for these things to kind of dock into the mother lode network but also undock and allow me as an individual to carry my computer up in the Yosemite backpacking and where there's you know no radio links and no fiber-optic links and still be able to use it and then come back and dock back into the network and find out what happened when I left and share some of my thoughts maybe with some other folks so we're working on that that's our goal for the next five years is that seamless transition between the standalone computer and the computer is part of this network community it also keeps away the Orwellian aspects of always being hooked into the net
p_segments.quote: There's always going to be that cabin in the middle of nowhere. If it doesn't work standalone, I'm not going to be happy.
p_segments.quoteCn: 总有那么一个偏远的小木屋。如果电脑不能独立工作，我会很不爽。

p_segments.question: 为什么好用的产品反而需要更强大的技术支撑？
p_segments.insight: Macintosh作为面向大众的易用电脑，揭示了一个悖论：为了实现极致易用性，反而需要消耗更多底层计算资源，使得易用产品在技术上比难用产品更强大。
p_segments.transcript: was that while experts could use some of the computers that were already out most people didn't want that again the computer was was not an end in itself it was a means to an end and so most people didn't want to learn how to use the computer they just wanted to use it and the Macintosh was supposed to be the computer for people to just wanted to use a computer without having to learn to use one spend six months now it turned out that the the paradox was that to make a computer easier to use you needed a more powerful computer in the first place because you were going to burn a lot of the cycles on making it easy to use and so this computer that was easy to use was actually more powerful and could do more things than the less easy to use computer and it
p_segments.quote: To make a computer easier to use, you needed a more powerful computer in the first place.
p_segments.quoteCn: 要让电脑更易用，你首先需要的是一台更强大的电脑。

p_segments.question: 为什么真正的创造者必须是思考者和行动者的结合？
p_segments.insight: 真正的创造者是思考者和行动者的统一体，他们不仅能构思宏大愿景，更能亲自动手解决实际技术难题，将艺术与科学融为一体，就像达芬奇一样，这是做出卓越贡献的关键。
p_segments.transcript: and I've seen a lot of people make a lot of things I've seen a lot of people fail a lot of things and my my point of view on this or my observation is that the doers are the major thinkers the people that really create the things that change this industry are both the thinker doer in one person and if we really go back and we examine you know did Leonardo have a guy off to the side that was thinking five years out in the future what he would paint or the technology he would use to paint it of course not Leonardo was the artist but he also mixed all his own paints he also was a fairly good chemist knew about pigments knew about human anatomy and combining all of those skills together the art and the science the thinking and the doing was what resulted in the exceptional result and there is no difference in our industry the people they've really made the contributions have been the thinkers and the doers and when you when you a lot of people of course it's very easy to take credit for the thinking the doing is more concrete but somebody is very easy for somebody say oh I thought of this three years ago
p_segments.quote: The doers are the major thinkers. The people that really create things are both the thinker and the doer.
p_segments.quoteCn: 实干者才是主要的思考者。真正创造事物的人，既是思考者也是实干者。

p_segments.question: 如何才能让一项新技术被社会广泛接受和普及？
p_segments.insight: 要推动新技术在社会中普及，最有效的方式是通过教育系统影响新一代人。随着代际更替，不接受新技术的老一代人逐渐退出，而从小接触新技术的年轻人会使其成为常态，从而实现广泛普及。
p_segments.transcript: you probably death is the best invention of life and because it means there's a constant turnover and so if you want to make a change in our society the best place to do it is in the educational system so that you're there there are now generations of people that have come out of school who computers are second nature to them and the people in our society that that at this point still have have not embraced these things or getting older and as that cycle that that wheel of birth and death turns just like driving people that don't drive are very rare and another generation or two people that don't use computers or be pretty rare going back March way of saying it but that's the it's very true I mean there's there is a line that says you those people that don't adopt it will die off well time passes yeah
p_segments.quote: Death is the best invention of life. If you want to make a change, the best place is the educational system.
p_segments.quoteCn: 死亡是生命最伟大的发明。如果你想改变社会，最好的地方是教育体系。

p_segments.question: 颠覆性创新是如何诞生的？为什么大公司会错失？
p_segments.insight: 微处理器最初被Intel设计为计算器组件，是业余爱好者社区率先将其发展为电脑，Intel在几年内都未能完全理解这一潜力。颠覆性创新往往源于边缘群体而非现有巨头。
p_segments.transcript: first figured out that the microprocessor was a computer they designed these things to be used in calculators and they thought the reason that the microprocessor came about was they thought if they design a slightly programmable one the next customer that walked in the door that wanted a slightly different calculator they could just spend a few months rather than a few years designing a new piece of silicon but I think the thought of making a computer never really occurred to them and it was the hobbyists that thought about making a computer these things it was this it was a computer hobbyist community that first did that and I don't think Intel quite understood that for a few years but again the first thing that happened was these people came together and formed a club the homebrew Computer Club
p_segments.quote: Intel figured out the microprocessor was a computer, but it was the hobbyists who thought about making a computer.
p_segments.quoteCn: 英特尔意识到微处理器就是一台计算机，但真正想把它做成电脑的是那些业余爱好者。

p_segments.question: 如何通过降低门槛来扩大产品市场？
p_segments.insight: Apple通过提供完全组装的电脑板（Apple I）和整机（Apple II），而非套件，极大地降低了用户门槛。这使得潜在用户从少数硬件爱好者扩展到数量级更多的软件爱好者，是Apple II成功的关键突破。
p_segments.transcript: so you needed to be pretty much of a hardware hobbyist now we made the very important decision was to not for our computers a kit even though you needed to buy these extra parts that the main computer board itself came fully assembled we were the first company in the world to do that everybody else was offering their little computers a kit and what that meant was was there was maybe an order of magnitude more people who could actually buy our computer and use it than if they had to build it themselves and the Apple 2 was actually the first computer to come fully assembled where you didn't have to do anything and the reason there was it was our observation that for every hardware hobbyist someone who could either build the kit themselves or at least find these five or ten extra parts they needed there were a thousand potential software hobbyists and if they didn't have to do anything with the hardware except use it ran at that time that meant write their own programs still there was a much
p_segments.quote: For every hardware hobbyist, there were a thousand potential software hobbyists.
p_segments.quoteCn: 每一个硬件爱好者背后，都有一千个潜在的软件爱好者。

p_segments.question: 杀手级应用如何成就一个平台？
p_segments.insight: Apple II的成功关键在于其可靠且廉价的软盘驱动器，以及足够大的内存（48KB），这比竞争对手领先2-3年。这使得VisiCalc（首个电子表格软件）只能在Apple II上运行，VisiCalc的出现反过来推动了Apple II的普及，奠定了其市场地位。
p_segments.transcript: development about this drive was crucial one of the things that people forget when they think about about Apple and the Apple 2 in particular was that that we were the first company to come out with a reliable inexpensive floppy disk drive and we had a low-cost floppy disk drive that really worked about two to three years before any of our competitors and that was an incredibly important reason why the Apple 2 was successful matter of fact there were a few others the Apple 2 could hold up to 48 kilobytes of memory which today doesn't seem like much but at that time was maybe three times as much as its competitors and that's why VisiCalc was written for the Apple 2 that was the only computer that could hold it and so if VisiCalc had been written for some other computer you'd be interviewing somebody else right now and it was because of that design decision and and other design decisions like it that the
p_segments.quote: If VisiCalc had been written for some other computer, you'd be interviewing somebody else right now.
p_segments.quoteCn: 如果 VisiCalc 是为别的电脑写的，你现在采访的就是别人了。

p_segments.question: 市场调研对突破性创新有什么局限性？
p_segments.insight: 市场调研无法预测用户对突破性产品的需求，因为它只能反映用户对现有产品或渐进改进的看法。真正的非增量创新需要直觉，市场调研应在产品原型出现后用于验证。
p_segments.transcript: it was less easy to do that and the problem is is that market research can tell you tell you what your customers think of something you show them or it can tell you what your customers want as an incremental improvement on what you have but very rarely can your customers predict something that they don't even quite know they want yet as an example no market research could have led to the development of the Macintosh or the personal computer in the first place so there are these sort of non incremental jumps that need to take place where it's very difficult for market research to really contribute much in the early phases of the thinking about how to you know what those should be however once you have made that jump possibly before the products on the market or even after is a great time to go check your instincts with the market place and verify that you're on the right track and usually when you show people something they'll say oh my god this is fantastic or you know give you some
p_segments.quote: Very rarely can your customers predict something that they don't even quite know they want yet.
p_segments.quoteCn: 客户很少能预测出他们自己都还没意识到想要的东西。

p_segments.question: 自动化生产的真正价值是什么？
p_segments.insight: 自动化生产的核心价值在于提升产品质量和缩短上市时间，而非单纯降低成本；这对于公司在技术密集型市场中生存和繁荣至关重要。
p_segments.transcript: quality is very important and the way we can make tremendous increase in quality and reductions in the time to market is through automation so the automation isn't there to lower the cost although it does do that it's really there to increase the quality and decrease the time it takes us to get our new product as an example to market which is very important in a technology-based marketplace so we happen to be the lowest cost producer in the world already at next of our class of products we also happen to be one of the highest quality producers of our type of product in the world and we think for a company to survive much less prosper in the 90s that these are going to be very very important things to be world-class at we're not competing at the homebrew
p_segments.quote: Automation isn't there to lower the cost. It's really there to increase the quality and decrease the time.
p_segments.quoteCn: 自动化的目的不是降低成本。它真正的意义是提高质量、缩短时间。

p_segments.question: 用户真正关心的是产品'能做什么'，而不是'怎么做'吗？
p_segments.insight: Macintosh的核心设计理念是让用户直接使用而非学习如何使用。用户真正关心的是产品'能做什么'，而不是'怎么做'，尤其年轻一代更看重结果而非实现细节。
